text: Queen’s University and the School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs welcome Indigenous students as part of the graduate community. A variety of funding opportunities are available at the university level and through programs across Canada to support Indigenous graduate students.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Indigenous students at Queen’s must self-identify in order to receive Indigenous-specific funding. Students are encouraged to consult the Registrar and Financial Aid Services website for more information about the self-identification process.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Bursary for Indigenous Students is awarded based on demonstrated financial need to Indigenous students in any year and any faculty or school at Queen’s University. Applications are submitted through SOLUS to the Office of the University Registrar, Financial Services.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Indigenous and Black Engineering and Technology (IBET) PhD Project supports underrepresented groups in STEM graduate studies in Canada. IBET recipients receive funding support and a Momentum Fellowship, which includes access to industry partnerships and additional networking opportunities.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Queen’s University allocates two Ontario Graduate Scholarship (OGS) awards each year specifically to Indigenous graduate students. Eligible applicants must self-identify on the OGS application to be considered. Typically, eligibility requires enrollment in a research-based program.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Students registered with their Indigenous community and holding a status card may be eligible for Band Funding to support postsecondary education. Each band administers funding independently with its own deadlines and eligibility requirements. Students are encouraged to contact the education representative at their band office to discuss application processes and timelines.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Queen’s University welcomes international graduate students and offers various funding opportunities from within the University, within Canada, and from external international sources to support their studies.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: A limited number of Ontario Graduate Scholarship (OGS) awards may be granted to international students holding a temporary resident visa study permit under the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act (Canada). The award is valued at $5,000 per term and must be held for two or three consecutive terms; one-term awards are not permitted. Applications are submitted directly to Queen’s.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Graduate Calendar’s External Awards Offered by Government and Other Granting Bodies provides further details about OGS and additional external funding opportunities available to international student visa holders at Queen’s.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: International students may apply for major national scholarships including the Vanier Canada Graduate Scholarships through one of the Tri-Council agencies (NSERC, SSHRC, CIHR). Additional opportunities include Government of Canada International Scholarships and Global Affairs Canada programs such as Study in Canada Scholarships, Scholarships and Educational Exchanges (SEED), and the Emerging Leaders in the Americas Program (ELAP). Universities Canada also administers government-funded international partnership programs.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Mitacs Globalink Research Award supports international research collaboration by providing $6,000 to assist graduate students with uncertain status in their current location to conduct research at Queen’s. Funding supports travel, accommodation, research-related expenses, and stipends for 12–24 week projects supervised by a Canadian faculty member. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Mitacs Globalink Graduate Fellowship provides $15,000 to former Globalink Research Interns who return to Canada for full Master’s, PhD, or postdoctoral programs at a Mitacs partner institution.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Further information about Canadian funding sources for international students is available through the Office of the University Registrar’s Student Awards and the Canadian Bureau for International Education.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: International students are encouraged to explore scholarship and funding opportunities offered by their home countries. Additional international opportunities may also be found through the VP Global Engagement website.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: CALDO supports recruitment and partnerships between Canada’s leading research-intensive universities and institutions in Central and South America. Through CALDO, Queen’s has agreements with CONAPE (Costa Rica) and SENACYT (Panama), with negotiations underway with FIDERH (Mexico) and Colfuturo (Colombia).

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Queen’s University has an agreement with the China Scholarship Council (CSC), a Chinese government agency providing scholarships to Chinese students for doctoral and postdoctoral studies abroad.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: CSC eligibility includes Chinese citizens studying in China or abroad who are fourth-year undergraduates, first-year PhD students, or current Master’s students. CSC scholarships are not available for Master’s degrees alone, but an offer leading to a PhD program may qualify.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Under the CSC agreement, the China Scholarship Council covers flights, accommodation, and living expenses for the student. The receiving department at Queen’s must provide funding at least equivalent to the cost of tuition, which may come from RA, QGA, TA, or other departmental resources. Supervisors are expected to cover bench costs where applicable.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Conditional letters of acceptance for CSC applicants must state that admission is contingent upon receiving the CSC Scholarship and that, upon confirmation, the student will receive funding equivalent to the cost of tuition fees.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Teaching Assistant (TA) assignments for CSC-funded students are determined by individual departments. TA stipends may form part of the tuition-equivalent award package, and students should consult their graduate coordinator for details.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: CSC applicants must first apply to a Queen’s graduate program through the SGSPA application portal and indicate in the Financial Information section that they have applied or intend to apply for the CSC scholarship, including the relevant CSC deadline. After receiving an offer of admission, applicants must apply to the CSC Doctoral Scholarship Program.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: CSC scholarship applications are submitted in two rounds: March 1–20 and May 20–30, with the second round dependent on remaining funds. Applicants whose prior degree was not taught in English must submit TOEFL or IELTS scores to Queen’s University. Further details are available on the CSC website.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Teyonkwayenawá:kon – Graduate Scholarship was established in June 2021 by the School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs to welcome new Indigenous graduate students to Queen’s. The scholarship is available to incoming Indigenous graduate students who self-identify at the time of application.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs
